title: Military Family: TSP Optimization and Deployment Planning
goals: Maximize retirement savings during combat zone deployment (tax-free window)
Optimize TSP contributions to full government match
Plan homebuying strategy around PCS frequency
Support Emily's career continuity across PCS moves
constraints: PCS moves every 2-3 years — homebuying complex
Emily's civilian career disrupted by military moves
Tyler's career decision: 20-year pension vs. earlier separation
Special military tax rules require specialized knowledge
key_risks: Combat zone deployment — life insurance adequacy is critical
TSP severely under-contributed for income level
PCS frequency destroys equity if buying/selling each move
Emily's income continuity risk with each move

planning_issues.description: TSP at 5% captures full match but misses combat zone Roth window — zero-tax Roth contributions possible during deployment
Combat zone tax exclusion creates rare Roth TSP opportunity: contribute after-tax funds in a year when income tax is near zero
BRS pension vs. civilian career decision; 20-year pension (40% of base pay) worth $624K present value at age 40
Homebuying strategy: VA loan benefit available but PCS every 2-3 years creates transaction cost trap

key_recommendations: Maximize Roth TSP during deployment ($23,000 limit) — zero-tax Roth contributions
Post-deployment: continue 15% Roth TSP contribution rate
Emily: increase 401k to 6% to capture full 3% employer match
VA loan planning for next PCS assignment if 3+ year stay
Model BRS pension value vs. earlier separation with max TSP
follow_up_questions: How many years has Tyler served? What is his anticipated career endpoint?
Does Tyler's deployment income exceed the annual TSP limit?
Does Emily have Military Spouse Preference for federal civilian employment?
Does Tyler have a VA disability rating from any prior injury?
outcome_summary: Tyler maximized Roth TSP during deployment ($18,500 in tax-free contributions). Emily increased 401k to capture full match. TSP balance grew to $68,000 in two years.
key_lesson: Military families have extraordinary financial benefits (TRICARE, SGLI, TSP matching, VA loan, combat zone tax exclusion) that are systematically underutilized. A deployment window is a rare opportunity to front-load Roth savings at near-zero tax cost.
